content:

Written After Spending A Day At West Point.
content:

title: The Vision Of Life.
content:

title: Lines, Addressed to the Young Gentlemen leaving the Academy at Lenox, Massachusetts.
content:

title: Written After Leaving West Point.
content:

title: The Red Indian.
content:
Rest, warrior, rest! thine hour is past, -

    Still rings upon the rushing blast,

title: To My Guardian Angel.
content:
Merciful spirit! who thy bright throne above
    Hast left, to wander through this dismal earth
    With me, poor child of sin! - Angel of love!

title: An Invocation.
content:

title: To A Picture.
content:

title: The Prayer Of A Lonely Heart.
content:
I am alone - oh be thou near to me,
    Great God! from whom the meanest are not far.
    Not in presumption of the daring spirit,

title: Song.
content:

title: Farewell To Italy.
content:
Farewell awhile, beautiful Italy!

title: To The Dead.
content:
On the lone waters' shore
         Wander I yet;
    Brooding those moments o'er
         I should forget.

title: Fragment.
content:

content:

title: A Retrospect.

title: Woman's Love.
content:

title: An Entreaty.
content:

title: Venice.
content:

content:

title: Lines For Music.
content:

content:

title: Written On Cramond Beach.
A Promise.
content:
By the pure spring, whose haunted waters flow
    Through thy sequester'd dell unto the sea,
         At sunny noon, I will appear to thee:

title: To Miss ---

title: An Evening Song.

title: An Apology.
A Wish.
content:

title: A Farewell.

content:

title: To Thomas Moore, Esq.
content:

title: To ----

content:

title: Eastern Sunset.
content:

title: To The Spring.
To The Wissahiccon.
content:
